things.name: Parliament Building
things.description: Stunning neo-Gothic parliament on the Danube — the world's third largest parliament building

things.name: Buda Castle & Castle Hill
things.description: UNESCO-listed castle complex housing the Hungarian National Gallery with sweeping city views

things.name: Szechenyi Thermal Baths
things.description: Magnificent neo-Baroque thermal bath complex in City Park, one of Europe's largest public spas

things.name: Fisherman's Bastion
things.description: Fairytale neo-Romanesque terrace with seven towers offering the finest views across the Danube

things.name: Great Market Hall
things.description: Budapest's largest and oldest indoor market (1897) with paprika, salami and Hungarian folk crafts

things.name: Hungarian State Opera House
things.description: Opulent neo-Renaissance opera house considered one of the most beautiful in the world

things.name: Ruin Bars of the Jewish Quarter
things.description: Szimpla Kert and other iconic ruin bars in crumbling pre-war buildings in the 7th district

things.name: Heroes Square & City Park
things.description: UNESCO-listed millennium monument with statues of Hungary's greatest historical figures

things.name: Dohany Street Synagogue
things.description: Europe's largest synagogue (1859) with a memorial garden and the Hungarian Jewish Museum

things.name: Danube Bend Day Trip
things.description: Scenic curve of the Danube north of Budapest with Visegrad Castle and charming Szentendre
